wiki.industry: fast food industry, fast food restaurant chain
wiki.extract: Dunkin' Donuts, doing business as Dunkin', is an American multinational coffee and doughnut company, as well as a fast food restaurant. It was founded by Bill Rosenberg in Quincy, Massachusetts, in 1950. The chain was acquired by Baskin-Robbins' holding company Allied Lyons in 1990. Its acquisition of the Mister Donut chain and the conversion of that chain to Dunkin' Donuts facilitated the brand's growth in North America the same year.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

sampleRecords.employer: National DCP, LLC / Dunkin Donut Distributor Center
sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was working inside the trailer and was pulling a loaded pallet with a manual pump jack to the side door. He lost his footing on the wet trailer floor and fell, resulting in a fractured right femur. The employee was hospitalized for surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by or caught in swinging door or gate
sampleRecords.final_narrative: At 12:30 p.m. on May 20, 2023, an employee was re-entering a building after taking out the trash when the door closed on his left ring finger. He suffered a fingertip amputation and was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by falling object or equipment, n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Employees were at site to plug a well and were pulling casing from the well and laying them on a skid. An employee who was standing on a metal bench/stool lost his footing. The case fell off the rack and struck him in the right leg, breaking his tibia and fibula. He was hospitalized and had surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by object falling from vehicle or machinery-other than vehicle part
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was laying down casing. The casing elevators opened and the 7" casing fell onto the employee. The employee sustained a fractured shoulder and ribs and head trauma.
